payload.text:
na loginscreen zmen text "Clamason Task Manager
v1.4.1" na 
"Clamason Task Manager
v1.4.2"

**Analyzing Application Updates**

I've determined the goal: updating the application version displayed on the `LoginScreen`. I've located the likely source for the text: the `login_title` key within `locales/common.ts`. Now, I'm verifying that this key is indeed the one being used and preparing to modify it.


**Confirming Text Location**

I've confirmed that `components/LoginScreen.tsx` indeed utilizes `t('login_title')` to render the title. I've examined `locales/common.ts` and found the version string, currently "v1.4.1", within the `login_title` key for both `sk` and `en` locales. I'm now certain that updating this key is the correct approach to modify the displayed application version. I have verified there are no other locations with the version. My plan is now concrete: Update `locales/common.ts` to change the version in `login_title`. I'm ready to make the file modifications.


**Examining Related Translations**

I'm now diving into the `locales/common.ts` file more thoroughly. I'm taking a look at related keys within the file. I have identified strings for subtitles, buttons, error messages, and even user interface navigation labels. I am focusing on what needs to be changed besides the current `login_title` key, and I am finding no updates are required beyond the version itself.


**Reviewing Additional Translations**

I am now thoroughly reviewing `locales/common.ts` to examine the other translation keys and to ensure I have not missed anything. I've focused on related translations and found many more strings. I've looked at tabs, permissions and more, but found no further updates needed beyond the version in `login_title`. I am now ready to change the version in the `login_title` key.


**Investigating New Keys**

I am now thoroughly reviewing `locales/common.ts`, examining newly identified keys and their usage. I've focused on strings related to "šrotu" ("scrap"), and have identified keys related to weighing, dispatching, and managing scrap metal inventory, including labels, buttons, and prompts.


**Exploring New Scrap Inventory**

I'm now investigating new keys within `locales/common.ts`, specifically focusing on those related to "šrotu" (scrap). I've found multiple strings concerning scrap metal inventory management, including labels for location, batch, quantity, and missing labels, as well as functions for adding items, exporting and finishing inventories, and starting inventory sessions.


**Modifying the Version String**

I've located the `login_title` key within `locales/common.ts` for both `sk` and `en` locales. I'm now updating the application version to "v1.4.2" within the key. I have updated the `en` translation. I will now examine the `sk` to ensure all key differences are accounted for.


**Reviewing Tabs & Permissions**

I'm now diving into the remaining `locales/common.ts` content. I'm focusing on new keys related to tabs and permissions within the app. I have identified keys that define new tabs for Logistics, Scrap, BOM (Bill of Materials), Missing Items, Logistics Center, Authorization, Part Catalog, Transactions (LOG), and Posting Error. Also, I am now focusing on permission keys related to ERP, Scrap Management, Weighing, and viewing/archiving scrap data. Finally, I have reviewed scrap related labels. I see no missing keys.


**Analyzing New Scrap Inventory**

I'm now deep-diving into the new keys added for scrap metal inventory within `locales/common.ts`. I see new strings related to weighing, dispatch, and archiving. I'm taking a close look at labels for "Netto (kg)", the bin and metal selection, dispatch date, and associated buttons ("SEND", "FINISH WEIGHING", "DELIVERY NOTE", "EXPEDITE SCRAP"). I've noted various titles, prompts, and states for weighing sessions and warehouses. I see labels for batch and inventory. I am confirming there is no related work required, and I'm ready to move on.

diagnostics.message: An object literal cannot have multiple properties with the same name.

payload.text:
**Analyzing the Error**

Okay, I'm now zeroing in on the file `locales/settings.ts` at line 167, as requested. The error's pointing to duplicate property names in an object literal. I'm going to carefully examine the code around that line to identify and resolve the duplicate keys. I will then update you with the precise fix.


**Pinpointing the Duplicate**

I've identified the root cause: a duplicated key, `perm_btn_block_new`, within the `locales/settings.ts` file. This duplicate is present in both the `sk` and `en` language sections. The initial line number was slightly off due to the snippet starting within the offending block. The task is now to remove the duplicate key, ensuring unique property names throughout. I will also be looking for other keys that are duplicated in other sections.

entries.description: Remove duplicate property 'perm_btn_block_new' in both 'sk' and 'en' locales to fix 'multiple properties with the same name' error.

entries.description: Vytvorenie novej podsekcie "QuickAction Architect" v nastaveniach ako placeholderu pre budúcu funkcionalitu.
Integrácia novej podsekcie "QuickAction Architect" do navigačného menu v nastaveniach.
Pridanie nového oprávnenia "perm_settings_qa_architect" do sekcie nastavení v rámci PermissionsTab.

payload.text: V nastaveniach vytvor novu podsekciu CustomerSupplierDatabase (Databaza dodavatelov  a zakaznikov) Budu tu dve databazy Zakaznici a Dodavatelia. V databaze sa to bude ukladat do kolekcie "CSDB" do jedneho dokumentu sa budu ukladat zakatznici ako addarray a do druheho dodavatelia. Pridavanie bude podobne ako pri dieloch hromadne a aj po jednom a to iste aj mazanie.Vytvor kolekciu a aj oba dokumenty v nej.  Vytvor na nu permisiu ako mame aj na ostatne sekcie v nastaveniach

**Considering Storage Structure**

I'm currently focused on the best storage strategy for the CustomerSupplierDatabase settings. I'm exploring how to efficiently manage the separate Customers and Suppliers lists. Considering separate lists might simplify access, while a unified approach could provide efficiencies. The specific data model is yet to be fully determined.


**Defining Data Structure**

I've decided on the database structure: a collection `CSDB` with two documents, `customers` and `suppliers`, both arrays of objects. My focus now is on implementing the functionality. This includes defining the `CSItem` type in `AppTypes.ts` and adding locales. I'll integrate a new permission, `perm_settings_csdb`, and hook into the settings UI.


**Implementing CustomerSupplier Feature**

I'm now in the thick of implementation. I've defined the `CSItem` type in `appTypes.ts` and added the necessary locale keys in `settings.ts`. Next, I'm working in `useMasterData.ts` and adding states, effects, and handlers for the CustomerSupplierDatabase (CSDB) logic, including event handlers for modifying customers and suppliers. Afterwards, I will expose the new functions via `useFirestoreData.ts`. Following that, I'll update the permissions tab and create the new CustomerSupplierSection.

Pridanie prekladov pre CSDB sekciu a oprávnenia.

**Analyzing Quick Actions**

I'm currently investigating why "Quick Actions" aren't initiating tasks in the "Rieši sa" status. It seems the `handleSubmit` function within `QuickActionTab.tsx` calls `data.onAddTask` as intended. I need to pinpoint where the status assignment is failing.


**Pinpointing Data Flow**

Okay, I've traced the data flow further. I confirmed that `addDoc` in `taskService.ts` *should* return a document reference, complete with an `.id`. Now, I'm examining `useTaskData.ts`. It appears `onAddTask` calls `taskService.addTask` and passes a constructed `taskData` object. I am checking if `status` is set correctly during this call.


**Examining Timing Discrepancies**

I'm now focusing on a potential timing issue. My latest hypothesis is that `onSetInProgress` might be running before the Firestore listener updates the local `tasks` state. This would explain why tasks aren't immediately showing as "in progress," even though `addDoc` is asynchronous and returns a document reference with an `.id`. My assessment suggests that the `Quota Guard` principle usually initiates "Quick Actions" immediately, so the delay is unexpected.


**Refining AddTask Function**

I'm now implementing the `autoStart` flag within `addTask` to eliminate the potential timing issue I've identified with `onSetInProgress`. This will directly include the 'in progress' status within the initial `addDoc` call, which would resolve the race condition and optimize the Quota Guard principles. The `useTaskData.ts`, `QuickActionTab.tsx` and `taskService.ts` files are being modified in the current iteration. I'll need to also update the `onAddTask` signature within `PartSearchScreen.tsx` to maintain consistency.


**Optimizing Quick Action Workflow**

I'm now iterating on streamlining the Quick Action process. The aim is to make the task start "immediately" and have no hardcoded values within `addTask`. I am updating `taskService.ts` to accept the `isInProgress` status, which simplifies the logic in `useTaskData.ts`. It will handle setting the flag based on a new boolean flag passed to `onAddTask` from `QuickActionTab.tsx`. This avoids the earlier timing issue I suspected and will likely optimize Quota Guard's behavior. The current draft includes updates to `taskService.ts`, `useTaskData.ts`, and `QuickActionTab.tsx`.


**Streamlining addTask Function**

I've streamlined the `addTask` function and removed the hardcoded values. I've updated `taskService.ts` to intelligently merge base defaults with the provided data, and accept a `startNow` flag. I'm now making modifications in `useTaskData.ts` to manage setting `isInProgress`, `inProgressBy`, and `startedAt` based on the new flag. The `QuickActionTab.tsx` component now correctly passes this flag. I've also found and corrected a typo in the previous implementation.

Máš pravdu, problém je v **race condition**. V predchádzajúcom kóde sa najskôr vytvorila úloha a následne sa hneď volala funkcia `onSetInProgress`. Táto funkcia však hľadá úlohu v lokálnom stave `tasks`, ktorý sa aktualizuje cez Firestore listener (`onSnapshot`). Ten nestihne zareagovať tak rýchlo, úloha sa v stave ešte nenachádza a funkcia skončí bez zmeny.

**Riešenie (v súlade s Quota Guard):**
Upravíme funkciu `onAddTask` tak, aby prijímala parameter `startNow`. Ak je pravdivý, polia `isInProgress`, `inProgressBy` a `startedAt` sa zapíšu **priamo pri vytvorení dokumentu**. Tým ušetríme jeden zápis (Write) a eliminujeme chybu v stave.

### Architektonické zmeny:
1.  **useTaskData.ts**: Rozšírenie `onAddTask` o parameter `startNow`.
2.  **QuickActionTab.tsx**: Volanie `onAddTask` s novým parametrom namiesto dvoch samostatných volaní.
3.  **PartSearchScreen.tsx**: Aktualizácia definície propov pre konzistenciu.

Fix the following errors:

```
Function addDoc() called with invalid data. Unsupported field value: undefined (found in field sourceSectorId in document tasks/Idm1mtdPYcdJjrUGfzLW)
```

**Identifying the Error Source**

I've pinpointed a Firestore error: `addDoc()` is failing due to an "undefined" value in the `sourceSectorId` field within a task document. It seems I accidentally introduced an unsupported `undefined` value, which Firebase Firestore rejects. This probably requires inspecting the data before the submission, and fixing the value when that is the case.


**Refining the Solution**

I've realized that Firebase Firestore accepts `null` or an omitted field, so my initial fix of intercepting `undefined` and replacing it is the wrong approach. The problem stems from how I'm handling the `src` and `tgt` parameters. Instead of explicitly setting `undefined`, I should allow the fields to be completely omitted when the source or target is null using the logical OR operator, instead of what is present in `hooks/data/useTaskData.ts`: `let finalSource = src || undefined;`
I'll focus on modifying `useTaskData.ts` to leverage this, as `taskService.ts` is correctly implemented.


**Addressing the Undefined Issue**

I'm now zeroing in on the root cause: explicitly setting fields to `undefined` in the object passed to `addDoc`. My primary goal is to ensure that target fields are either `null` or omitted when empty. While I initially tried to avoid `null` to resolve a TypeScript build error, this seems to be the right way to approach Firestore. My plan is to use conditional object building within `hooks/data/useTaskData.ts` to only include the field if it has a non-empty value. I'll need to double check the type and QuickActionConfig to ensure type safety.


**Refining Field Handling**

I'm now zeroing in on a cleaner approach. Instead of explicitly setting fields to `undefined`, I'll conditionally include `sourceSectorId` and `targetSectorId` in the data object passed to `addDoc` in `useTaskData.ts`. This involves initializing the object without these optional keys, and then adding them only if they have a non-null and truthy value. I want to keep the TypeScript type definitions sound, and Firestore happy. For new tasks, I will omit the keys. I've re-examined `taskService.ts` to ensure compatibility.


**Evaluating Null vs Undefined**

I'm now reconsidering the use of `null` versus omission of the fields. While null is preferable for analytics consistency in the database, it might reintroduce the TypeScript build errors if the code expects `string | undefined`. After checking `types/appTypes.ts`, I see that the `Task` interface defines `sourceSectorId` and `targetSectorId` as optional, which should accommodate this approach. I'm implementing a conditional approach in `useTaskData.ts` to add `sourceSectorId` and `targetSectorId` only if a value is present, avoiding explicit `undefined` assignments. I also found and reviewed another instance of using null vs undefined and confirmed it is using `string | null` and is fine.


**Eliminating Undefined Values**

I'm now focusing on removing all occurrences of `undefined` within the data objects. My current approach involves conditionally adding the `sourceSectorId` and `targetSectorId` properties to the `taskData` object in `hooks/data/useTaskData.ts` only when they have actual values. I'm extending this approach to `components/settings/QuickActionArchitectSection.tsx` and the `onAddQuickAction` function in `hooks/data/useMasterData.ts`, to ensure consistency across the codebase.

**Implementing Null/Undefined Stripping**

I'm now focusing on a utility, or inlined logic, to strip `undefined` values before sending data to Firestore. I'll focus on `useTaskData.ts` and `useMasterData.ts` to implement this. My plan is to conditionally add the `sourceSectorId` and `targetSectorId` keys if non-null in the data object passed to `addDoc`. The null in `isMissing` is okay.


**Refining Null Handling**

I'm now revisiting the interface updates, opting for a minimal fix. I'll ensure that hooks like `useTaskData.ts` and `useMasterData.ts` never include keys with `undefined` values. The `null` values will be allowed in the database, and `types/appTypes.ts` doesn't need to change.

I'm now focusing on a robust fix for `useTaskData.ts` and `useMasterData.ts`, to ensure keys with `undefined` values never appear in the data objects passed to Firestore. I've switched to a conditional approach using the spread operator, selectively including `sourceSectorId` and `targetSectorId` only when they have non-null values. This avoids the pitfalls of explicitly setting `undefined`. I'm extending this fix to `onAddQuickAction` in `useMasterData.ts` using the same approach.

I'm now zeroing in on resolving type definitions in `types/appTypes.ts` to accommodate `null` values where UI fields can be empty. I need to make sure that these fields can accept `null` to align with the updated data-stripping logic, avoiding `undefined` values that cause Firestore errors. I'll focus on the `sourceSectorId`, `targetSectorId`, `defaultText`, `completedBy`, and `missingReason` fields. This change should resolve the Vercel build errors.


**Correcting Interface Types**

I'm now zeroing in on resolving Vercel deployment and `addDoc()` errors. I'll update `types/appTypes.ts` to allow `string | null` for optional fields, aligning the database schema with how the UI handles empty fields. I'll ensure `hooks/data/useTaskData.ts` fixes `onAddTask` to use `null` for empty values. This is the simplest fix.
